2604.19488.title: CoDA: Towards Effective Cross-domain Knowledge Transfer via CoT-guided Domain Adaptation
2604.19488.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved substantial advances in logical reasoning, yet they continue to lag behind human-level performance. In-context learning provides a viable solution that boosts the model's performance via prompting its input with expert-curated, in-domain exemplars. However, in many real-world, expertise-scarce domains, such as low-resource scientific disciplines, emerging biomedical subfields, or niche legal jurisdictions, such high-quality in-domain demonstrations are inherently limited or entirely unavailable, thereby constraining the general applicability of these approaches. To mitigate this limitation, recent efforts have explored the retrieval of cross-domain samples as surrogate in-context demonstrations. Nevertheless, the resulting gains remain modest. This is largely attributable to the pronounced domain shift between source and target distributions, which impedes the model's ability to effectively identify and exploit underlying shared structures or latent reasoning patterns. Consequently, when relying solely on raw textual prompting, LLMs struggle to abstract and transfer such cross-domain knowledge in a robust and systematic manner. To address these issues, we propose CoDA, which employs a lightweight adapter to directly intervene in the intermediate hidden states. By combining feature-based distillation of CoT-enriched reference representations with Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) for kernelized distribution matching, our method aligns the latent reasoning representation of the source and target domains. Extensive experimental results on multiple logical reasoning tasks across various model families validate the efficacy of CoDA by significantly outperforming the previous state-of-the-art baselines by a large margin.
2604.19488.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在邏輯推理方面取得了顯著進展，但仍然落後於人類的表現。上下文學習提供了一種可行的解決方案，通過用專家策劃的、領域內的範例來促進模型的性能。然而，在許多現實世界中缺乏專業知識的領域，如低資源科學學科、新興生物醫學子領域或小眾法律管轄區，這種高質量的領域內示範本質上是有限的或完全不可用的，從而限制了這些方法的普遍適用性。為了減輕這一限制，最近的研究探索了檢索跨領域樣本作為替代的上下文示範。然而，所得到的增益仍然有限。這主要歸因於源分佈和目標分佈之間顯著的領域轉變，這妨礙了模型有效識別和利用潛在共享結構或潛在推理模式的能力。因此，當僅依賴原始文本提示時，LLMs在穩健和系統性地抽象和轉移這種跨領域知識方面面臨挑戰。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了CoDA，它使用輕量級適配器直接介入中間隱藏狀態。通過將基於特徵的CoT增強參考表示的蒸餾與最大均值差異（MMD）進行核化分佈匹配，我們的方法對齊了源領域和目標領域的潛在推理表示。在多個邏輯推理任務上進行的廣泛實驗結果驗證了CoDA的有效性，顯著超越了先前的最先進基準，取得了大幅度的提升。
2604.19488.author: Jianzhi Yan et.al.
2604.19488.authors: Jianzhi Yan, Le Liu, Buzhou Tang, Yang Xiang, Dongning Sun, Zhiming Li
